paper.title: Evolutionary Map of the Universe: Detection and Analysis of the Shell Surrounding the Runaway Wolf-Rayet Star WR16

extraction.summary: The paper studies the runaway Wolf-Rayet star WR16 and its circumstellar shell. WR16 is described as a runaway star with a peculiar tangential velocity of ~50 km/s, but the paper does not assess whether it is bound or unbound to the Galaxy, nor does it present WR16 as a possible Galactic-unbound or hypervelocity star candidate. No other objects are discussed as potentially escaping the Milky Way.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The runaway Wolf-Rayet star WR16, for which the paper reports Gaia proper motions and a peculiar tangential velocity of ~50 km/s.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper describes WR16 as a runaway star but does not present it as possibly unbound from the Galaxy. No bound/unbound assessment is performed, and the velocity is consistent with a Galactic runaway rather than a hypervelocity or unbound star.

source_refs.context: paper title describing WR16 as a runaway Wolf-Rayet star
measurement of peculiar velocities and tangential velocity, with no bound/unbound discussion

candidate_groups_considered.description: Internal observation log table listing radio flux densities for WR16 and its nebulous shell.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The table contains only radio flux density measurements and a spectral index; it does not list stellar candidates or kinematic data relevant to Galactic escape assessment.

source_refs.context: table caption and contents showing radio flux densities
